content.verdict: Vanila Hotel & Spa is the mid-luxe boutique on Nosy Be itself — 32 rooms and suites set in a tropical garden 200m from the long white-sand Andilana Beach, on the quietest western tip of the island. Unlike the private-island lodges of the Mitsio and Radama archipelagos, Vanila is genuinely on the main island of Nosy Be — meaning no speedboat transfer, no satellite-internet limitations, and a rate point that makes Madagascar accessible to honeymoon couples who want the country's lemur-and-beach combination without the all-inclusive private-island price tag. The hotel's main appeal is the location: Andilana Beach is widely considered the prettiest beach on Nosy Be (kilometres of fine white sand, swimming-pool-blue water, no rocks, no current), and the surrounding northwestern tip is far quieter than Hellville town or the Madirokely tourist strip on the east coast. Vanila's owners (a French-Malagasy couple, longtime Nosy Be residents) have refined the property over twenty years; the spa is a serious operation with three open-air pavilions, the kitchen runs a Mediterranean-Malagasy menu with proper wine cellar, and the boat-charter desk arranges every excursion in the archipelago — Nosy Tanikely, Nosy Iranja, Nosy Komba, the Mitsio. For couples doing a Madagascar trip with one private-island leg and one mainland leg, Vanila is the ideal Nosy Be base.
content.best_room: The Pool Suites (rooms 28–32) include a private plunge pool, expanded outdoor terrace, and the most privacy in the garden — these are the dedicated honeymoon category. Junior Suites 18–22 sit closest to the spa pavilions. Standard Superior rooms are perfectly comfortable but lack the outdoor space; for a 7-night honeymoon, the Pool Suite upgrade is worth the +$200/night.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Tana Arrival
itinerary_7_nights.description: Land at Antananarivo (TNR) via Paris (Air France direct), Mauritius, Nairobi, or Addis Ababa. Overnight transit at Relais des Plateaux near Ivato. First Malagasy dinner of zebu carpaccio, grilled rougeret, vanilla-rum dessert. The 06:30 Tsaradia morning hop tomorrow.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Tsaradia & Andilana Arrival
itinerary_7_nights.description: 90-minute flight to Nosy Be (NOS), 45-minute road transfer across the island to Andilana Beach on the northwest tip. Welcome ti-punch and immediate first swim — Andilana is the longest white-sand stretch on Nosy Be. Light lunch at the pool grill, settle into the Pool Suite, and a beach-served dinner with paraffin lanterns at sunset.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Nosy Komba Lemur Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Full-day boat excursion to Nosy Komba — the Lemur Island in the Nosy Be channel — with a guided rainforest walk to the habituated black-lemur troops at the village of Ampangorina, a Malagasy lunch with the village cooperative, and an afternoon snorkel at the south reef. Return to Vanila at golden hour.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Nosy Tanikely Marine Reserve
itinerary_7_nights.description: Boat trip 30 minutes to Nosy Tanikely — the protected marine reserve with green turtles, eagle rays, and barracuda schools on the snorkel reef. Beach lunch at the lighthouse cove, an afternoon climb to the lighthouse for the panoramic view, and snorkeling on the south reef before returning.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Nosy Iranja Twin-Island Sandbar
itinerary_7_nights.description: An ambitious full-day excursion 90 minutes south by speedboat to Nosy Iranja — the two islands joined by a 1km tidal sandbar. Walk the sandbar at low tide, lunch in the village, snorkel the south reef, and return for sunset. The lodge's most photographed day-trip.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa, Beach & Lokobe Forest
itinerary_7_nights.description: Slower day. Morning at Lokobe National Park (the rainforest pocket on the southeast of Nosy Be) — guided pirogue paddle, black-lemur tracking, panther chameleon spotting. Lunch back at the hotel, an afternoon spa double-treatment in the pavilion (signature Vanille de Madagascar ritual), and a private dinner on the beach.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Swim & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: A morning swim at Andilana, a long breakfast, and the road transfer back to Nosy Be airport for the early-afternoon Tsaradia to Tana. Connect to the evening Air France 22:00 to Paris or the 16:00 Air Mauritius to Mauritius. The hotel handles all transfer legs.

caveats: Vanila is on Nosy Be itself, not a private island — meaning the beach is shared with neighbouring resorts and a small village; not the total exclusivity of Tsarabanjina or Nosy Saba.
Antimalarial prophylaxis is recommended for Madagascar — consult a travel-medicine clinic 6 weeks before travel.
Internet is hotel-grade (faster than the private-island lodges) but not always reliable; aim for email-only, not video calls.
Vanila has Booking.com and Expedia inventory, but for honeymoon stays the direct booking via vanila-hotel.com or specialist agents is preferred — better room category control.
Cyclone season (mid-Jan to mid-March) brings genuine weather risk; aim for May–November for reliable beach conditions.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Vanila is the most accessible romantic stay on Nosy Be — a Pool Suite at $600/night gets you a private plunge pool, the prettiest beach on the island, and the full Nosy Be archipelago at your doorstep for excursions. For honeymoon couples on a $5,000–7,000 budget for the week, Vanila is the value pick.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: May through November is dry season with the most reliable beach conditions. September and October are peak. Avoid mid-January through mid-March (cyclone season — operational but weather-disrupted).

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: The Pool Suites (rooms 28–32) book 3–4 months ahead in honeymoon high season. Andilana Beach in general fills quickly Christmas, Easter, and August (Italian school holidays); aim for a 4-month lead time.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: Vanila welcomes families and the property does see Italian and French families in August; for the quietest honeymoon experience, aim for May–June or September–October when the guest mix skews adult.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: A Pool Suite (rooms 28–32) is the honeymoon recommendation — private plunge pool, expanded terrace, and the most privacy in the tropical garden. Junior Suites 18–22 are the next category up; standard Superior rooms work for shorter stays but lack the outdoor space for a full week.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Fly to Antananarivo (TNR) overnight from Europe, connect on Tsaradia (90 minutes) to Nosy Be (NOS), then a 45-minute road transfer across the island to Andilana Beach on the northwest tip. Vanila arranges all transfers.
